$note: Canonical Website Services Agreement. SINGLE SOURCE OF TRUTH. Consumed by build-proposal.py (renders the on-page review block + sign.html) and by api/complete-signature.js (renders the executed PDF). Placeholders {AGENCY} {COMPANY} {CLIENT} {CLIENT_LOCATION} {DATE} {PAYBODY} {JURISDICTION} are filled at render time. Replace with your own solicitor-reviewed contract before using for real deals. Liability, indemnity and availability clauses are a starting template, not legal advice.
title: WEBSITE SERVICES AGREEMENT
sections: This Website Services Agreement (the "Agreement") is entered into between {AGENCY} (the "Provider") and {COMPANY}{CLIENT_LOCATION_CLAUSE} (the "Client"), together the "Parties", effective as of {DATE}.

The Provider will design, build, and launch a custom website for the Client as presented in the accompanying proposal, including hosting setup, lead-handling configuration, and search-optimisation foundations, followed by the ongoing monthly service plan selected in the proposal.

The setup fee compensates the Provider for immediate commencement of design, build, and configuration work and for access to the Provider's proprietary systems and processes. Accordingly, the setup fee is earned on receipt and is strictly non-refundable once work has commenced. The monthly service plan may be cancelled per Section 6 and no refunds are issued for any period already invoiced.

The Provider guarantees that the website will meet the scope agreed in the accompanying proposal. If any deliverable falls short of that scope, the Provider will remedy it through continued work at no additional cost until it meets the agreed scope. This continued-work remedy is the Client's sole and exclusive remedy for any shortfall in the deliverables and does not entitle the Client to any refund of the setup fee or of any fee already invoiced.

The monthly service plan continues month to month. After the third month the Client may cancel with fourteen (14) days written notice ahead of the next billing date. The Provider may suspend services for non-payment.

Upon payment in full of the setup fee, the finished website content specific to the Client is licensed to the Client. The Provider retains ownership of its underlying systems, templates, tooling, and know-how.

To the maximum extent permitted by law, the Provider's total aggregate liability arising out of or in connection with this Agreement, whether in contract, tort (including negligence), or otherwise, is limited to the total fees actually paid by the Client to the Provider under this Agreement. The Provider is not liable for any indirect, incidental, special, consequential, or reputational loss, loss of profit, loss of revenue, loss of goodwill, or loss of data, however arising. Nothing in this Agreement limits liability that cannot be limited by law.

The Client is responsible for all content, materials, credentials, and instructions it supplies to the Provider, and warrants it has the right to use them. The Client will indemnify and hold the Provider harmless against any claim, loss, or expense arising from the Client's content, the Client's use or operation of the website after handover, or the Client's breach of this Agreement. The Provider is not responsible for the unlawful acts of third parties, including any unauthorised access, defacement, or interference with hosting or accounts controlled by the Client.

The website is hosted on third-party platforms. The Provider does not guarantee uninterrupted or error-free availability and is not liable for outages, suspensions, price changes, or failures of any third-party platform, domain registrar, email provider, or payment processor. The Client is responsible for securing its own accounts, including enabling two-factor authentication where available.
This Agreement is governed by the laws of {JURISDICTION}, and the Parties submit to the exclusive jurisdiction of its courts.
